disclaimer: Unofficial fan-made artist notes for Waking Life 2026. Not affiliated with festival organizers.

2JACK4U.bio: Lisbon duo of DIY acid-techno producers and live performers (Acid Mary aka Rubina and Jack Drop aka André), celebrating the analog sound with spontaneous, improvised hardware sets.

69db presents Wave Arising.bio: Founding member of Spiral Tribe and R-Zac, co-founder of Network 23 Recordings and a key innovator of the 90s Hardtec/Tribe scenes. Pushing live-improvised hard techno since 1991; recent project 'Wave Arising' blends ambient, tribal, experimental and hard techno, named one of the standout performances at Dekmantel 2024 by Resident Advisor.

Alan Braxe.bio: French producer and DJ associated with the late-1990s French touch sound; co-creator of Stardust's 'Music Sounds Better With You' and founder of Vulture Music.

Aleksi Perälä.bio: Finnish electronic composer and producer known for the Colundi sequence, intricate microtonal or frequency-based melodic systems, and a vast catalogue of crystalline techno experiments.

Alexi Perälä.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Alton Miller.bio: Detroit producer, percussionist and DJ connected to the city's early house and techno history, known for warm, soulful, rhythmically rich deep house.

Amelia Holt.bio: New York-based DJ and NTS resident known for agile, atmospheric sets that connect techno, breaks, electro and leftfield club music.

Andrés Zacco.bio: Argentinian producer and DJ associated with detailed, rolling techno and minimal forms, with releases on labels such as Ilian Tape and Greener.

Ani Zakareishvili.bio: Georgian DJ and producer connected to Tbilisi's underground club scene, with sets that move through techno, electro and sharp-edged experimental dance music.

Asmaa Hamzaoui & Bnat Timbouktou.bio: Southern Moroccan desert. Gnawa as living memory. Stories of slavery, migration, spiritual endurance carried in a woman's voice that could crack stone. Desert blues meets trance-state invocation.
Asmaa Hamzaoui & Bnat Timbouktou.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Ata Kak.bio: Ghanaian vocal acrobatics. Syllable-bender. Smile-generator since '94. Discovered on Awesome Tapes from Africa. Sounds like surfing on pure sunshine.
Ata Kak.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

BASHKKA.bio: Munich-born DJ and producer of Turkish heritage, shaped by a decade in New York's ballroom scene. Blitz Club resident and Panorama Bar regular; debut EP 'Maktub' (2023).

Bonga.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Carmen Villain.bio: Carmen Villain is the recording project of Carmen Hillestad, a Norwegian-Mexican artist known for hazy ambient, dubwise electronics, woodwinds and spacious experimental composition.

Carrier & Gavsborg.bio: Carrier is the dub-influenced bass project of producer Guy Brewer; Gavsborg is co-founder of the Equiknoxx crew, known for off-kilter experimental dancehall.

Cassy.bio: DJ, producer and vocalist raised between Vienna and the UK, with a long history at Panorama Bar and clubs worldwide; a respected deep house and techno voice for two decades.
Cassy.sourceLabel: model_knowledge (artist identity corroborated by second research pass)

CCL.bio: Seattle-raised, Berlin-based DJ known for mind-bending, genre-fluid bass-led sets that move between breaks, dub and trippy club music.
CCL.sourceLabel: model_knowledge (genre framing corroborated by second research pass)

Charlemagne Palestine, Oren Ambarchi & D.bio: One of the founders of the NYC school of minimalism music reimagines his 1997 meditative performance Karenina. Colourful consonance swelling until you can't tell where the room ends and your bloodstream begins.
Charlemagne Palestine, Oren Ambarchi & D.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Coco Maria.bio: Selectora and radio host from Monterrey, based in Amsterdam. Hosts 'Club Coco' on NTS, specialising in Brazilian, Latin American and Caribbean wax.
Coco Maria.sourceLabel: ra + official site (corroborated by both passes)

Colleen presents Libres Antes del Final.bio: Moog Matriarch-led pulse work by Colleen, with immersive, ripple-like synth movement.

Cosmo.bio: Hyojin Min, a rising DJ from Seoul (active since 2017, BBCB resident) now based in Germany and aligned with the Giegling label. Dreamy, gleaming sets criss-crossing psychedelic disco, minimal acid and meandering '90s techno, mostly below 125 BPM; made her production debut with the aqueous house/techno EP 'JIN 09'.
Cosmo.sourceLabel: festival organizer confirmation + provided RA bio

Dana Kuehr.bio: Chicago-born DJ and producer, now based in Brussels. Sundaze resident; released the acclaimed 'Basic Moves 18' double EP and appears on Die Orakel. Rugged electro with wiggly basslines.

Dasha Rush.bio: Artist based between Berlin and Paris, running Fullpanda Records. Moves between stripped-down techno and poetic ambient/AV performances (Raster-Noton releases).
Dasha Rush.sourceLabel: model_knowledge + ra (corroborated by second research pass)

Decius.bio: "This so fucking tough. On first listen, I got that feeling you get when you've been driving through the pitch black desert, finally hearing the bass echoing in the distance, and you make a big turn which may actually just be a cliff, but instead ends at a rave with people going off, lasers beaming between the dunes, and you know you're in for a wild night." - the greatest bandcamp review we ever read.
Decius.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Devon Rexi.bio: Amsterdam-based group around Nicolini (drums/vocals) and Nushin Naini (bass/vocals): motorik kraut-rock mentality with dub-laden physicality and playful experimentation. Part of the South Of North scene.
Devon Rexi.sourceLabel: ra + label/agency pages (confirmed by both passes)

Dj Godfather.bio: Detroit legend Brian Jeffries, pioneering ghettotech specialist. High-octane turntablism fusing electro, Miami bass and booty house; runs Databass Records.

Dj Marcelle.bio: DJ Marcelle / Another Nice Mess (Marcelle van Hoof): a fearless, playful pioneer who DJs and produces on three turntables at once, layering environmental sounds, heavy bass, post-punk, dub, weird hip hop and new African dance music. Releases on Klangbad (Faust) and Jahmoni.

DJ Trystero.bio: DJ from the Kansai scene known for deep, unpredictable vinyl sets; a favorite in the international minimal circuit.

Djrum.bio: London producer and turntablist Felix Manuel, celebrated for emotional, genre-fluid productions and virtuosic three-deck vinyl sets. Releases on Houndstooth. An exploratory improvised live set featuring bespoke instruments made here by the lake.

e/tape.bio: Sound researcher. Deep thinker. Local fixture. Colours his worlds lush, ethereal, diffused. Soft edges to hold onto as the sun comes up.

Eduardo de la Calle.bio: Madrid-based producer and DJ with a vast hardware-driven catalogue ranging from lush soundscapes to deep, hypnotic techno (Cadenza, Analog Solutions).

Edward.bio: Gilles Aiken, Berlin DJ and producer making shadowy club tracks with a surrealistic, cosmic edge - house and techno echoing Brian Eno, Robert Fripp and Conny Plank (whom he has remixed). A mainstay of Berlin's WHITE label and Weimar's Giegling crew; latest album 'Into A Better Future'.

Edward & Konstantin.bio: Gilles Aiken, Berlin DJ and producer making shadowy club tracks with a surrealistic, cosmic edge - house and techno echoing Brian Eno, Robert Fripp and Conny Plank (whom he has remixed). A mainstay of Berlin's WHITE label and Weimar's Giegling crew; latest album 'Into A Better Future'.

Flo Masse.bio: DJ who came up through Rio de Janeiro's scene and is now based in Barcelona, with French-Brazilian roots. Energetic vinyl sets weaving house, acid and trance, informed by a drummer's sense of groove; founded the Pulso label.
Flo Masse.sourceLabel: web + soundcloud (Brazil corroborated by both passes)

Força Maior.bio: Two men. A 400-year-old Portuguese farm where revolution was once plotted. Out came not gunpowder but a saxophone stitched into a tapestry both delicate & intense.

Haruka.bio: Tokyo-based DJ, resident of the legendary Future Terror parties alongside DJ Nobu and a leading figure in Japan's dance music scene; deep, spiraling techno sets.
Haruka.sourceLabel: ra (Tokyo base and psychedelic-techno framing explicit per second pass)

Heith & Tarawangawelas present Duori.bio: Born in jaipong clubs at 2am, cigarettes dying in ashtrays, someone muttering about ghosts. A friendship stretched across twelve time zones. The distance itself became the music.
Heith & Tarawangawelas present Duori.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

HHY & The Kampala Unit.bio: Brass voltage. Mutant Electronics. Deep soundsystem pressure. Steam. Served Kampala hot. Tastes like nothing you've ever heard.
HHY & The Kampala Unit.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Holy Tongue.bio: Psychedelic, free-form, high energy, spiritual dub-dance music.
Holy Tongue.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

I:Cube.bio: Nicolas Chaix records as I:Cube, a long-running French producer on Versatile Records whose work spans deep house, electro, disco mutations and cinematic electronics.

Jan Jelinek.bio: German electronic musician whose work as Jan Jelinek, Farben and Gramm helped define a warm, crackling strand of minimal electronics, sample-based ambient and dub-techno abstraction.

Jane Fitz.bio: London DJ with 30+ years behind the decks, beloved for long-form, subtly psychedelic all-vinyl journeys. Co-runs Night Moves and Dream Bags parties.

Jichael Mackson.bio: German producer known for playful, melodic minimal house and microhouse with a dubby, late-night touch.

John McEntire & Sam Prekop.bio: Thirty years defining post-rock's skeleton. Tonight they shed skin. Rhythm-driven electronica and modular pieces so precise they make you want to dance and meditate simultaneously. Sit still. Move. Both at once.
John McEntire & Sam Prekop.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Joshua Serafin & Mario Barrantes Espinoz.bio: Fractured Divinities meets Relics meets Maxhete. Exactly.
Joshua Serafin & Mario Barrantes Espinoz.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: medium)

Katatonic Silentio.bio: Milan-based producer, DJ, sound artist and independent researcher in sound cultures, exploring murky bass, broken rhythms and industrial-leaning club music (Ilian Tape, Mille Plateaux).

Keith Worthy.bio: Detroit-rooted producer, DJ and Aesthetic Audio founder whose music is known for raw, soulful deep house and techno with a strong underground sensibility.

Kia & Richard Akingbehin.bio: Kia is the founder of the Animalia label and Cirrus, based in Melbourne, known for fluid, aquatic, psych-leaning selections; Richard Akingbehin is a British-Nigerian, Berlin-based DJ, label head and Refuge Worldwide co-founder with a dub-techno depth.

Kiss Nuka.bio: Anushka Manchanda: artist, producer and activist based between Mumbai and Barcelona, with an artistic legacy from Bollywood playback singing to film. Genre-bending live electronic sets weaving roots, growling bass, lush soundscapes and socio-political storytelling focused on nature and the rise of the feminine.

Kitty Daisy & Lewis.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Konduku.bio: Producer Ruben Üvez, of Turkish heritage, crafting spacious, hypnotic, polyrhythmic tracks on Nous'klaer Audio and Spazio Disponibile.
Konduku.sourceLabel: ra + horst (Dutch identity and Turkish roots explicit per both passes)

Kuba'97.bio: Brussels DJ and producer chasing the 'trance dance experience': 90s tribal techno meets contemporary psychedelia. Kiosk Radio regular, involved in Horst and Stelplaats.

Lawrence & Thomas Melchior.bio: Thomas Melchior's microhouse/minimal vocabulary (Perlon, Playhouse) back-to-back with Hamburg Dial co-founder Lawrence's deep, melodic house-and-techno language: two veterans in dialogue.
Lawrence & Thomas Melchior.sourceLabel: model_knowledge + ra (both identities confirmed by second pass)

Leafar Legov.bio: Rafael Vogel records as Leafar Legov, a German artist associated with Giegling, known for intimate, melancholy ambient, house and minimal techno.

Lerosa.bio: Leopoldo Rosa records as Lerosa, an Italian-born, Dublin-based producer known for melodic electro, acid, deep house and techno on labels such as Lunar Disko and Further.

Lip Sync 4 your Waking Life.bio: The sequel. If you were there last year, you know what madness to expect. If you weren't... well.. you kinda had to have been there.. ;) FYI, this is also your moment to shine. Yeah you :)

Lula Pena.bio: One guitar. A single thread tugging you through fado's exquisite grief, flamenco's gut-punch, bossa nova's tenderness. A poet who sings and a singer who poems. Lisbon's quiet earthquake.
Lula Pena.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

mad miran.bio: Rotterdam-rooted, Amsterdam-associated DJ celebrated for fearless, fast-moving genre-resistant sets that flip between broken beats, jungle, trance and oddities; De School alumna and festival favorite.
mad miran.sourceLabel: model_knowledge + ra editorial (NL corroborated by both passes)

Magda & Mike Servito.bio: Polish-born, Detroit-raised DJ/producer who rose through Richie Hawtin's M_nus camp; runs Perm Records. Precise, playful minimal techno, with projects reaching into art and tech. | Detroit-bred cult DJ now based in New York; longtime resident of The Bunker NY and a master of raw, sweaty acid-house sets.
Magda & Mike Servito.sourceLabel: model_knowledge | ra (Detroit-rooted framing explicit per both passes)

Mala.bio: Pioneer Mark Lawrence from South Norwood, London; half of Digital Mystikz and co-founder of DMZ and Deep Medi Musik. Played a defining role in dubstep before it even had a name.
Mala.sourceLabel: ra + official site (South Norwood explicit per second pass)

Marylou.bio: Berlin-based DJ and Morphine Records & Ominira affiliate with impeccable, daring taste. Her psychedelic, no-boundaries sets have reached Terraforma, Intonal, CTM and Cafe Oto, and she hosts shows on Rinse France and Refuge Worldwide.

Matthias Reiling.bio: German producer and DJ, one half of Session Victim, known for warm, sample-laced house music, deep grooves and live-leaning club productions.

Mia Koden.bio: London artist and DJ formerly associated with Sicaria Sound; her solo work as Mia Koden focuses on dark, percussive UK bass pressure, dubstep weight and grime-informed club music.

Michael J Blood.bio: Manchester producer associated with improvised, hardware-shaped house and techno, often released anonymously or with little biographical framing.

Michelle Gurevich.bio: When poetry meets music in a smokey bar; melancholic, dramatic, and strangely comforting at the same time.
Michelle Gurevich.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Millie McKee.bio: London-based multi-disciplinary artist, record collector and DJ; manager of the Hidden Sounds record store and NTS resident, playing deep, spiritual and psychedelic selections.

Mohammad Reza Mortazavi.bio: His fingers don't play the tombak and daf, they whisper to them, argue with them, bring to life unmapped cartographies. A metronome that dreams.
Mohammad Reza Mortazavi.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Natalia Escobar.bio: Colombian artist and DJ whose sets and productions move between ambient, experimental electronics, slow club music and Latin American musical references.

Nídia & Valentina Magaletti.bio: Syncopated drums, marimba lines, warm synths. Dance music with pools of personality, swagger and soul.
Nídia & Valentina Magaletti.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Nightmares on Wax.bio: Long-running project of George Evelyn from Leeds, a Warp Records mainstay known for smoky downtempo, sample-rich trip hop, soul, dub and mellow dance music.

NikNak.bio: Leeds-based turntablist, composer and sound artist; first Black turntablist to win an Oram Award. Immersive, sample-rich composition and improvisation.
NikNak.sourceLabel: ra + artist bios (UK explicit per second pass)

Niño de Elche.bio: Flamenco's beautiful heretic. For twenty years he's been unpicking flamenco's seams, rewiring its soul.
Niño de Elche.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Nkisi.bio: 'Music for Magic' ain't no metaphor, but a living technology for transformation. It's an insurgency against agreed-upon reality. Rhythms that rupture the mass hypnosis. Perceptual trapdoors swinging open.
Nkisi.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Nosedrip.bio: Ziggy Devriendt from Ostend, founder of the Stroom label and radio platform; a digger with wide-ranging, eccentric taste in forgotten Belgian and global music.

o ghettão.bio: Ever heard polyrhythmic afro-deep-house, kuduro and minimal povera jazz all in one place? Well Lisbon based DJ Nigga Fox, DJ Danifox and DJ Firmeza are gonna bring it.

O Ghettão.bio: Ever heard polyrhythmic afro-deep-house, kuduro and minimal povera jazz all in one place? Well Lisbon based DJ Nigga Fox, DJ Danifox and DJ Firmeza are gonna bring it.

Ojoo.bio: Brussels-based DJ (aka OJOO GYAL), selector, organiser and community builder of Moroccan origin, channeling North African rhythms into genre-defying, bass-conscious club sets.
Ojoo.sourceLabel: ra + agency/horst (origin, base and style explicit per second pass)

Olgica.bio: DJ and promoter based in Graz, Austria; part of the Interlude crew and founder of ISKRA in Belgrade. Patient, immersive genre-free journeys.
Olgica.sourceLabel: ra + web (Serbia explicit on Elevate/MENT/Kiosk bios)

Om Unit Acid Dub Studies.bio: Jim Coles' Om Unit project performing material from Acid Dub Studies: weighty dubwise electronics that connect classic soundsystem pressure with jungle, techno and contemporary bass music.

Otto Landsberg & Shin Hyo Jin.bio: Afro-cubano chekeres amplifying the sounds of Cuban resilience. The jing: a deep Korean gong swallowing silence like an egg eating snake.

Paquita Gordon.bio: Vinyl-focused DJ who moves freely across styles, drawing inspiration from travel, ritual and collective experience; co-founder of the Tempio del Futuro Perduto project in Milan.
Paquita Gordon.sourceLabel: model_knowledge + ra/horst (approach explicit per second pass; country from model knowledge)

Parallel 9.bio: Deep, hypnotic alias of Amsterdam techno veteran Steve Rachmad, explicitly listed among his aliases on RA; classic 90s Parallel 9 material was reissued on Delsin.
Parallel 9.sourceLabel: ra + web (alias confirmed by both passes)

Party Smith.bio: Lisbon DJ active in the local scene with appearances at Arroz Estúdios, Carpintarias de São Lázaro and the ELA collective's Earth Night events.

Pedrinho Xalé.bio: Arrived in Lisbon at 18 with Santiago Island lodged in his marrow. Turned longing luminous. Synthesizers bent to Cape Verdean soul. Reggae and funaná tangled into something no shoreline can contain.

Polar Inertia.bio: French techno project known for immersive, cinematic live performances that pair austere deep techno with ambient interludes and spoken-word science-fiction atmospheres.

Prosumer.bio: Achim Brandenburg, better known as Prosumer, is a German DJ and producer associated with Panorama Bar and a deeply musical approach to house, disco and techno.

protodub.bio: Alias of Vincent Vidal, a soundsystem ambassador and low-frequency architect moving between spatial audio, PA engineering and live mixing. Founder of the Subwise sessions and the Headroom soundsystem in Lisbon; affiliated with Studio DB Berlin.

Rene Wise.bio: DJ/producer championed by Ben Klock; groove-centric, looping techno fusing detail and long-form storytelling, on Mote-Evolver and his own Moment label.
Rene Wise.sourceLabel: ra (British identity strongly supported per second pass)

Rhadoo.bio: Co-founder of [a:rpia:r] with Raresh and Petre Inspirescu; a foundational figure of the Romanian minimal sound and one of its most revered selectors.

Robert Aiki Aubrey Lowe.bio: Modular synth alchemy and longform timbral architecture from the Brooklyn-based composer.

rRoxymore.bio: Berlin-based producer/DJ making playful, percussive and exploratory club music drawing on musique concrète, free jazz and Afrofuturist ideas (Don't Be Afraid, !K7).

Sam Prekop.bio: Two members of Chicago's The Sea and Cake: Sam Prekop's melodic modular language with John McEntire's compositional and production craft (duo album 'Sons Of' on Thrill Jockey).
Sam Prekop.sourceLabel: bandcamp + official site (collaboration explicit per both passes)

Samuel Rohrer & Eivind Aarset.bio: Drums that listen. Guitar that shapeshifts into vapour. Hazy, nocturnal, wandering like a half-remembered dream.
Samuel Rohrer & Eivind Aarset.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Serenne.bio: DJ and producer; regular at Zurich's Supermarket Club, has played Closer (Kyiv) and Guesthouse (Bucharest), collaborates with Giegling and runs the Interlude label.

Shackleton.bio: Sam Shackleton is a British producer known for hypnotic, percussion-heavy bass music, from Skull Disco's dubstep-era mutations to later ritualistic techno and experimental live work.

Shahzad Ismaily.bio: Multi-instrumentalist. Heartstring plucker. If this doesn't leave you tender, check your pulse.
Shahzad Ismaily.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Simply Rockers Sound System.bio: Roots and dub soundsystem session; believed to be the Lisbon-based Simply Rockers hi-fi crew (unconfirmed - verify with the festival).

SnPLO.bio: SnPLO pairing: PLO Man (Berlin-based) with fellow countryman and longtime collaborator DJ Spence, focused on deep, dense and exploratory dub-techno dancefloor music.

Sonic Interventions.bio: Diasporic-futuristic, jazzified big-bang of music, spoken word and movement.

Takkak Takkak.bio: DJ Scotch Egg meets Raja Kirik in a collision named after the sound of a wooden fish being struck. Industrial. Joyful. Completely unclassifiable. The name says it all and nothing, you'll have to feel it for yourself.
Takkak Takkak.sourceLabel: program image + AI knowledge (genre/country confidence: high)

Tau Car.bio: DJ and producer now based in Berlin; regular at Club der Visionäre, The Pickle Factory and Waking Life. Vibrant, hazy house with the Risky Bookings crew.

Tiago.bio: Lisbon DJ and producer Tiago Miranda, a cornerstone of the local scene (Lux Frágil) with releases on DFA and Ene Records.

Tobias..bio: Confirmed as Tobias Freund: highly regarded producer, sound engineer and musician (aliases Pink Elln, Tobias.). Decades of experimental, precision-engineered electronic music; a master of analog gear and improvisational live performance.

Tristan Arp.bio: Producer, DJ and composer born in suburban Detroit, now based in London (via Mexico City). Co-founder of the Human Pitch label with Simisea and Lot Radio resident; weaves modular synthesis, cello, hand-drumming and spoken word across club and ambient iterations (Wisdom Teeth, 3024, Banoffee Pies).

Ulf Eriksson.bio: Swedish DJ, producer and label figure associated with Kontra-Musik, known for deep, exploratory selections across techno, house and experimental club music.

Upsammy x Gyrofield.bio: Gyrofield is a Hong Kong-born producer pushing technoid drum & bass and exploratory club pressure; Upsammy (Thessa Torsing) is known for crystalline, fluid IDM and broken techno.

Valody.bio: DJ, producer, promoter and driving force behind the Elberec label; Disturbia Amsterdam resident. Originally from Portugal, now based in the Netherlands.

Vedic Roots.bio: Roots/dub collaboration session; Vedic Roots is believed to be a Portuguese roots reggae outfit joining the Simply Rockers soundsystem (unconfirmed - verify with the festival).

Vera, DJ Dustin, Mimi, Vuur, ....bio: Vera Heindel, long associated with Frankfurt's scene and labels like Freebase and Half Baked; a gifted selector with flawless technique and an ego-free, story-first approach. | Amsterdam DJ Guido Janssen, IsBurning resident and Bar Pamela programmer; sexy, groove-driven house sets, Boiler Room debut at DGTL 2023.
tags: subtle vinyl-led club music | Groovy house
Vera, DJ Dustin, Mimi, Vuur, ....sourceLabel: ra + agency (style and Germany explicit per second pass) | ra + web

VIL.bio: Nuno Costa, Lisbon DJ and producer; co-founder of the Hayes Collective. Syncopated, Detroit-leaning techno with releases on Synewave NY, Faut Section and Hayes; a regular at Lux Frágil, Gare and Ministerium.
VIL.sourceLabel: ra + insomniac + death techno + hayes bandcamp

Vlada.bio: A master of musical finesse: mischievous, seductive selections at Panorama Bar, Houghton and Waking Life, and deeper, more obscure experimental sets at Berlin Atonal and Draaimolen. Sleek yet raw, dark yet shimmering; also curates the SpeLL and dreamslesssweet projects. Russian-born, Berlin-based.
Vlada.sourceLabel: festival organizer confirmation + provided bio (origin per Ekipa/RA sourcing)

XDB & Lowtec.bio: Pairing of German underground artists XDB and Lowtec, both known for patient, dubby, deeply textured house and techno rooted in small-label dance music culture.

Yamour.bio: Berlin-born vinyl-only DJ who honed her knowledge working at Hard Wax; rooted in American house and techno, with gigs at Blitz, Giegling parties and beyond.
